title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
At the "Joyful Coffee Shop," Poirot discusses Jane (also referred to as Jenny or Madame Jenny) with Phryne, Phoebe, and Phyllida. Phyllida defends Jenny’s taste in tea, while Polly inquires about the location of the house where Jenny works. Phryne explains that Jenny was deeply wounded by a single, undisclosed event years ago, which caused her to live in her own world. Phoebe describes Jenny as extremely self-absorbed and lacking curiosity about others or social gossip. Phyllida adds that while Jenny does not dwell on herself, she keeps her own unhappy secrets. Concluding that Jenny is in danger, Poirot vows to return the following morning to find her before harm arrives, though Phryne expresses doubt that anyone can truly find her.

Edward Catchpole, a thirty-two-year-old friend of Poirot from Scotland Yard, provides a retrospective account of the events. He recounts meeting Poirot six weeks prior, when Poirot was planning a temporary stay at Mrs. Blanche’s apartment to allow his "fast-working" brain to rest. During this time, Poirot revealed he is Belgian rather than French. On Thursday, February 7, 1929, Poirot visited the "Happy Café" because his landlady, Mrs. Eversleigh, was away. Catchpole notes that Poirot returned home late, around ten past ten, after meeting Jenny. Catchpole also observes that Mrs. Eversleigh appeared anxious upon her return and noted that Catchpole himself looked unwell.
